what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that documents a severe, compounding design risk: the fail-loud frontmatter parsing in `get_item` can cause a single malformed file to drop a node out of the graph entirely.
observations: Item id is item-bbc7b3b0 and address is board://item-bbc7b3b0
Type is block, source is claude_code, state is current
Channel is dragnet-development and it links via part_of to board://item-48cd6801
Title labels the issue 'P5 · 1.3 Frontmatter-mangle meets fail-loud → cascading unreadability (SEVERE, compounding)'
Body states `get_item` raises `BoardError` when `id` is missing or unparseable
Argues that fail-loud behavior is correct discipline but brittle against a sloppy co-writer
Warns that one YAML parsing break can remove a node from the master graph until a human fixes it
Cites 265 files and a phone-driven mobile-sync writer as realistic context
Frames the failure as a credibility-eroding, half-working mode that 'Tim cannot afford'
standouts: The tension between 'fail-loud is correct discipline' and 'brittle against sloppy co-writers'
A single malformed file can make a node vanish from the master graph
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses Markdown body with a numbered subsection heading and inline bold emphasis
concerns: No concrete reproduction example or sample malformed YAML is provided
Severity is asserted without quantitative evidence
Refers to 'Tim' without context that would help an unfamiliar reader
notes: A severity-flagged noticeboard item arguing that fail-loud frontmatter parsing in `get_item` risks making nodes silently disappear when mobile-sync writers produce malformed YAML.
questions: What exactly does `get_item` do when frontmatter is malformed versus when `id` is merely missing?
What do 'P5' and '1.3' refer to in the title?
Who is Tim and what is his role in this project?
What is the content or purpose of the linked item-48cd6801?
What remediation or graceful degradation is being proposed?
purpose_vs_actual: yes — the file documents the stated severe compounding risk as a noticeboard item.
intention_signals: It appears to be raising awareness that strict frontmatter validation, while disciplining, creates a single-point-of-failure where malformed files drop out of the graph, likely to motivate a more resilient load path or quarantine mechanism.
novelty: It frames correctness-oriented fail-loud error handling as a liability rather than a virtue in a multi-writer, mobile-sync environment.
contribution: Records a concrete design risk linking strict frontmatter parsing, mobile-sync authoring, and node disappearance, preserving the rationale for future resilience decisions.
summary_for_embedding: A dragnet-development noticeboard item documenting a severe compounding risk that fail-loud frontmatter parsing in `get_item` can cause a single malformed file to vanish from the master graph when co-writers edit on mobile.
intention_for_embedding: To flag that strict BoardError handling of missing or unparseable item ids creates brittleness against malformed YAML from mobile-sync writers and erodes system credibility.
